oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Remembering Terry Timberlake
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Franco, Madeleine
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: This item originally appeared in AFA Watchbird Volume 49 Number 3 (April 2022).The following is an excerpt from the full PDF link.Lynne Sherrill (Terry) Segal TimberlakeOctober 21, 1945 to March 26, 2022 by Madeleine FrancoAFA has lost a grand champion and a nonpareil.Terry’s business card identified her as “Exotic Bird Broker,” and that she was in more ways than one! Often, it was difficult to discern whether exotic referred to the birds she shipped all over the world or to the woman herself. It was both! Few, if any, who knew her would argue otherwise. Lots can be said about this fashion model turned charter boatswain turned bird broker.Long before Taylor Swift had her posse, Terry had mastered the art of collecting a cadre of friends whose interests meshed with her own. She cultivated many enduring friendships through her businesses, in more recent years brokering and often personally delivering the most exotic of the exotics. Dozens, if not hundreds, of friends; I’m just one of them. There were the breeders, the vendors, the zoos, both public and private, import agents, cargo carriers, various airline personnel and retail customers . . . “bird people” of every stripe. And, of course, there were especially her “Palmie Mommies,” those who acquired palm cockatoos through Terry, many of us “repeat offenders” who wouldn’t acquire an exotic bird any other way. And, many of those knew one another because Terry loved to share her friends. Her enthusiasm was unstoppable, her advocacy amazing, her networking enviable.Click the PDF link associated with this item to view the complete item as originally published.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 49 No. 3 (2022): Watchbird April 2022; 8-11
